messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\ShwNodApp\public\js\components\expenses\ExpenseModal.tsx:70:17
  68 |             if (expense) {
  69 |                 // Edit mode - populate with expense data
> 70 |                 setFormData({
     |                 ^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  71 |                     expenseDate: expense.expense_date?.split('T')[0] || '',
  72 |                     amount: expense.amount || 0,
  73 |                     currency: (expense.currency || '').trim() || 'IQD',

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [showAlert]

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'onChangesUpdate'. Either include it or remove the dependency array. If 'onChangesUpdate' changes too often, find the parent component that defines it and wrap that definition in useCallback.

suppressedMessages.message: Reads via fetchJSON/apiLoader must pass a Zod guard `{ schema: <contract>.response }` — it is the only fail-loud response validation in prod (the server sendData parse is dev-only). For a deliberately schema-less read (literal-null signal, raw passthrough, status ping, fire-and-forget) add an inline // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax with a reason. (Shared-contract lock-in — D3.)

suppressedMessages.message: Use the helpers in core/http.ts (fetchJSON/postJSON/putJSON/deleteJSON/postFormData) instead of bare fetch() — they add credentials, throw on !ok, and unwrap the success envelope. (Audit H1 funnel.)

React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'getShareFileName', 'handleNativeShare', 'personId', 'toast', and 'togglePhotoPrivacy'. Either include them or remove the dependency array. If 'setShareSources' needs the current value of 'personId', you can also switch to useReducer instead of useState and read 'personId' in the reducer.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [loading, images, getShareFileName, togglePhotoPrivacy, handleNativeShare, toast, personId]

fix.text: [loading, images, getShareFileName, togglePhotoPrivacy, handleNativeShare, toast, personId]

suppressedMessages.message: Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Avoid non-native interactive elements. If using native HTML is not possible, add an appropriate role and support for tabbing, mouse, keyboard, and touch inputs to an interactive content element.
Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Avoid non-native interactive elements. If using native HTML is not possible, add an appropriate role and support for tabbing, mouse, keyboard, and touch inputs to an interactive content element.
The autoFocus prop should not be used, as it can reduce usability and accessibility for users.
The autoFocus prop should not be used, as it can reduce usability and accessibility for users.
The autoFocus prop should not be used, as it can reduce usability and accessibility for users.
Use the helpers in core/http.ts (fetchJSON/postJSON/putJSON/deleteJSON/postFormData) instead of bare fetch() — they add credentials, throw on !ok, and unwrap the success envelope. (Audit H1 funnel.)

suppressions.justification: returns a PDF blob (res.blob()) + reads X-Total-* response headers; needs the raw Response (so it bypasses core/http.ts's envelope unwrap). The funnel also attaches the CSRF token on mutations, so we replicate that here (audit H2) — staffCsrfProtection 403s tokenless /api mutations.

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

messages.message:
Error: Cannot access variable before it is declared

`loadContacts` is accessed before it is declared, which prevents the earlier access from updating when this value changes over time.

  62 |         // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps

suppressedMessages.message: Reads via fetchJSON/apiLoader must pass a Zod guard `{ schema: <contract>.response }` — it is the only fail-loud response validation in prod (the server sendData parse is dev-only). For a deliberately schema-less read (literal-null signal, raw passthrough, status ping, fire-and-forget) add an inline // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax with a reason. (Shared-contract lock-in — D3.)

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  58 |     useEffect(() => {
> 59 |         setHasAnimated(true);
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  60 |     }, []); // Empty deps = run once on mount
  61 |
  62 |     // Format SQL Server TIME value (HH:MM:SS) to 12-hour format

messages.message:
Compilation Skipped: Use of incompatible library

This API returns functions which cannot be memoized without leading to stale UI. To prevent this, by default React Compiler will skip memoizing this component/hook. However, you may see issues if values from this API are passed to other components/hooks that are memoized.

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'initializeEditor' and 'toast'. Either include them or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [initializeEditor, toast]

suppressions.justification: intentional: init runs ONCE on mount. The container div is always rendered, so it's available on first run. `toast` is stable from context. Depending on `isLoading` here is a bug: load() toggles isLoading for the spinner, which would re-fire this effect, run the cleanup, and destroy the editor it just created (leaving getHtml() to crash on a torn-down canvas frame).

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'loadTemplateContent'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suppressions.justification: intentional: loadTemplateContent reads `template` (already a dep) plus stable refs

suppressedMessages.message: Reads via fetchJSON/apiLoader must pass a Zod guard `{ schema: <contract>.response }` — it is the only fail-loud response validation in prod (the server sendData parse is dev-only). For a deliberately schema-less read (literal-null signal, raw passthrough, status ping, fire-and-forget) add an inline // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax with a reason. (Shared-contract lock-in — D3.)

suppressions.justification: raw literal-null signal; no contract response

suppressedMessages.message: Reads via fetchJSON/apiLoader must pass a Zod guard `{ schema: <contract>.response }` — it is the only fail-loud response validation in prod (the server sendData parse is dev-only). For a deliberately schema-less read (literal-null signal, raw passthrough, status ping, fire-and-forget) add an inline // eslint-disable-next-line no-restricted-syntax with a reason. (Shared-contract lock-in — D3.)

suppressions.justification: session ping; no payload to validate

suppressions.justification: bespoke WhatsApp-send API client (parallel to core/http.ts): adds retry w/ exponential backoff (RetryManager), per-request AbortController cancellation, and validateApiResponse. Reconcile with core/http.ts once it gains retry (M8) + abort (M7).
